title: AI-Powered Creative Writer & Storyteller вҖ“ My Journey Back to Passion
tags: AI, writing, creativity, storytelling, personal, growth, poetry
snippet: Stepping Back into Eden: A PoetвҖҷs Authentic Story of Passion, Exile, and RedemptionDiscovering My Literary Paradise: Writing as a  Young PoetOnce, I lived in a literary paradise. I discovered my lo...
content: Stepping Back into Eden: A PoetвҖҷs Authentic Story of Passion, Exile, and RedemptionDiscovering My Literary Paradise: Writing as a  Young PoetOnce, I lived in a literary paradise. I discovered my love for writing in high schoolвҖ”a love so deep, it felt like magic. My beloved, the Word, and I were like two dreamers, rowing a paper boat with a pencil as our oar, across the boundless ocean of imagination. Together, we found the Garden of Eden.During my student years, I dwelled in a sacred paradise of words. In that haven, I was Adam, and the Word was my Eve. Together, we wove beauty, crafting poems and stories that breathed life into my dreams. But one day, temptation whispered:вҖҳLet us taste the forbidden fruit.вҖҷDrawn to the Tree of Work, I bit into its fruitвҖ”the relentless pursuit of money. In that single act, I severed my bond with the Word. My paradise crumbled, leaving me exiled in a harsh world far from my literary dreams.Falling into the Corporate Trap: Losing My Writing PassionLeaving university felt like leaving Eden. My passion for writing in Azerbaijani TurkicвҖ”a language marginalized in IranвҖҷs education systemвҖ”offered no path to a stable livelihood. Few people could read or appreciate Azerbaijani literature, leaving my words to echo unheard and my dreams out of reach.Out of necessity, I strayed from the right path. I chased money, mistaking it for the ultimate success, unable to see it as merely a reward for pursuing our passion. In doing so, I abandoned the Word and stepped into the unrelenting grind of corporate life.That was my original sinвҖ”the moment I lost my way. I became an exile, trapped in a world of endless routines, drained of joy and creativityThe Search for Purpose: Failing to Replace My Creative FireAs the weight of routine grew unbearable, I promised myself IвҖҷd break free within two years. But two years turned into ten. I became a вҖңhell boy,вҖқ trapped in a soul-crushing office that suffocated my spirit and stifled my creativity.Desperate to escape the monotony, I strayed further from my true passion, wandering through the endless search for something new:  I tried language teaching, but it failed to ignite a spark.  I explored Python programming and data science, only to quit.  I ventured into web development, hoping it might lead me to fulfilment.Each path taught me lessons but left me hollow, unable to replace the fire of my first love. Without faith in my true callingвҖ”and consumed by the struggle to make a livingвҖ”I kept straying further, repeating my sin. I chased substitutes for my passion, only to feel more lost and disconnected from the path I was meant to walk.Rediscovering My True Calling: Writing as My Path to FreedomEach detour whispered the same truth:вҖңEvery path is strewn with struggles and obstacles. ThereвҖҷs no shortcut to success, no escape from the need for time and patience. But only on your true path will passion sustain you.вҖқOne day, an inner voice awakened me:вҖңWithout repentance, youвҖҷll remain lost. Challenges will come, but if you return to your true calling, passion will be your companion, helping you persist through every trial.вҖқMy diversions werenвҖҷt failuresвҖ”they were lessons. They taught me that persistence alone isnвҖҷt enough; it must be rooted in passion. And passion was waiting, all along, on the path I had strayed from. With humility, I repented and took the first step back toward my rightful path, ready to embrace its struggles.Returning to Writing: Finding Redemption in CreativityAmid the corporate grind, I finally found my way back to the Hidden Garden of Eden. Slowly and patiently, I returned to my first love: writing.I began to heal myself with the 3 Ps: passion, patience, and persistence. Each step felt like therapy, reminding me that the journey was the paradise IвҖҷd been searching for all along.As Adam reunited with Eve, I reentered my Eden. This time, I refused the forbidden fruit of chasing money for its own sake. Instead, I wrote for the joy of creationвҖ”for the beauty of weaving words into worlds.Now, I walk this path with passion, patience, and persistence, finding happiness in every step.A Message to Creatives: Follow Your Passion with PatienceTo all who seek their true path: step boldly forward. The road will teach you to walk, stumble, and rise again. Embrace each challengeвҖ”they are the rhythms of your sacred journey.Welcome, fellow pilgrim. Like you, IвҖҷve wandered far and returned to my first loveвҖ”words. IвҖҷm walking alongside dear companions like language learning, exploring the storyland where creativity blooms.This website is my sanctuaryвҖ”a garden where I nurture my dreams as an Azerbaijani poet, writer, and storyteller. Here, I invite you to join me in:  Journaling My Journey: Sharing reflections, struggles, and victories.  LearNotes: Insights from language learning and storytelling.  Creating Stories and Poems: Tales that inspire, educate, and spark imagination.Follow my journey. Together, weвҖҷll explore a world where passion lights our way, creativity shapes our future, and every step forward is a reward. LetвҖҷs build a storyland where words inspire, teach, and unite us all.

title: How discovering Markdown Transformed My Learning and Note-taking Journey.
tags: coding, learning, note-taking, markdown, markup, markup-language, web, code, chronicles, active, learning
snippet: uring my рҹ…ҝпёҸython learning journey, I initially used Microsoft Word for note-taking, but I found it time-consuming and ineffective.Notion seemed promising with its creativity, innovation, and AI too...
content: uring my рҹ…ҝпёҸython learning journey, I initially used Microsoft Word for note-taking, but I found it time-consuming and ineffective.Notion seemed promising with its creativity, innovation, and AI tools, but I realized I was spending more time on templates and design than on learning Python рҹҗҚ.hile everyone emphasized the importance of note-taking, my existing methods were wasting precious time. I would watch tutorials, take notes, and never revisit themвқ—Feeling stuck, I shifted to solely watching tutorials, until I encountered a breakthrough.ecently, during my Git learning on DataCamp, I faced a dilemma вҖ“ I wasnвҖҷt taking notes. The README.md file in my Git repository caught my attention. An idea struck:  use GitHub as a notebook and Markdown as the note-taking tool. And so, my first note,вҖңIntroduction to GitвҖқ, was born, accessible HERE. ARKDOWNв¬ҮпёҸhas empowered me to create organized notes and actively learn Git. Now, IвҖҷm immersed in the practical application of Markdown, simultaneously learning and documenting my progressвҖҰрҹҡ¶рҹҸјLetвҖҷs start the journeyрҹ§іWhat is Markdown?в“ӮпёҸрҹ…°пёҸRKDрҹ…ҫпёҸWN is a sweet and suitable way to write content for the webрҹ•ёпёҸ. It is a рҹӘ¶lightweight markup language with plain-text formatting syntax. It uses symbols like #, *, _, >, and ! to format text, create headings, lists, paragraphs, and more. Markdown is ideal for web content, documentation, and note-taking due to its simplicity and readability.Table of Contents: {#top}            1. Headers      9. рҹ“ғLists                  2.Text Formatting      10. Scaping Characters              3.Paragraphs & Line Breaks      11. Tables              4. Blockquote      12. Heading IDs              5. Horizontal Lines      13. Footnote              6. Code Blocks      14. Best Markdown Editors              7. рҹ”—Links      15. Conclusion              8. рҹ–јпёҸImages      В       в“ӮпёҸarkdown В©пёҸheat Sheetрҹ’ЎBefore starting, open a Markdown Editor; copy or write each syntax there to see the magic рҹӘ„1. eaders	# First Level Header	## Second Level Header	### Third Level Header	#### Fourth Level Header	##### Fifth Level Header	###### Sixth Level Headerрҹҹ°вһ–Alternate Syntax for H1 & H2:	First Level Header	==================	Second Level Header	-------------------Tip:      Put blank lines before and after a heading.    Add a space between # and the heading text.    Use headings for hierarchy, not just for styling.  2. Text formatting            Format      Syntax      Output                  Bold      **text** or __text__      Bold Text              Italic      *text* or _text_      Italic Text              Bold & Italic      ***text*** or ___text___      Bold and Italic              Strikethrough      ~~text~~      Strikethrough              Highlighted      ==Highlight==      ==Highlight==              Subscript      <sub>text</sub>      Subscript              Superscript      <sup>text</sup>      Superscript      3. Paragraphs & Line BreaksUse 2пёҸвғЈSPACEs(вҳ„пёҸвҳ„пёҸ) at the end of a line to insert a line break.Press ENTER to start a new paragraph.This is the first line.вҳ„пёҸвҳ„пёҸ This is the second line.вҳ„пёҸвҳ„пёҸThis is a new paragraph.вҳ„пёҸвҳ„пёҸ4. BlockquoteвқҮпёҸBlockquotes are created using the > symbol.> This is a blockquote.>> - You can include multiple lines.> - Even lists inside!вқҮпёҸNested Blockquotes:> This is an outer blockquote.>> This is an inner blockquote.>>> You can go deeper.5.Horizontal LinesUse 3пёҸвғЈthree or more asterisks (***), dashes(---), or underscores(___):***---___6.Code Blocksрҹ’ Inline codeвң…To make codes  visible as they are, we wrap the them in 2пёҸвғЈ backticksВ `<br> `рҹ’ Code Blocksвң…To create Code Blocks, use 3пёҸвғЈ backticks(```) or tilde(~~~)before and after the code snippet, and provide the language name after initial backtics, in order toВ Highlight the code syntax.   ```html     Code Block goes here...     ```7.рҹ”—Links:рҹ–ҮпёҸInline link:[MY GitHub](https://github.com "GitHub Homepage")**My GitHub_рҹ–ҮпёҸReference link:[My Linkedin] [ refrence-to-link ][ refrence-to-link ]: https://www.linkedin.com/in/pirikli/ "Pirikli"вһЎпёҸMy LinkedInрҹ–ҮпёҸDirect Link:<pirikliartan@gmail.com>{:target="_blank"}вһЎпёҸ pirikliartan@gmail.comрҹ’ЎTip: We add the{:target="_blank"}immediately after the link, in order to open the link in new tab.8. рҹ–јпёҸImages:рҹ“·Inline Image:![Alt text](image-url.jpg "Image Title")рҹ“·Reference Image:![Alt text][image]    [image]: image-url.jpg "Image Title"9. рҹ“ғListsрҹ”ўOrdered list1. This is the first item of this ordered list.2. This is the second item.3. The third item.вҸ№пёҸUnordered list:* This is the first item of this unordered list.  * This is the second one.* and son on...рҹӘәNested lists(indentation in lists):вң…Use TAB to indent nested lists1. The first item with inner list:    - first inner    - second inner2. The second item with nested list    * first inner    * second inner        - one        - two            * A            * BTask Lists:- [ ] Wake Up- [ ] Get Up- [ ] Wash Up- [ ] Rush Up10. Scaping Characters:To display Markdown symbols as plain text, use a backslash (\).\*This text is not italicized\*Here is the list of other characters to scape.11. Tables| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 ||----------|----------|----------|| Data 1   | Data 2   | Data 3   || Data 4   | Data 5   | Data 6   |12. Heading IDsI think of it as вҖңInner LinkвҖқ or вҖңIN Page LinkвҖқ. I usually use it to create вҖңContent TableвҖқ and вҖңрҹҰҳJump Up HighвҖқ.[Here is the Link to that Heading](#headid5)         ##### This is a heading we want to refer or link to {#headid5}13. FootnotesThis is a sentence with a footnote.[^1][^1]: This is the footnote text.14. Best Markdown Editors: Why Obsidian Stands OutMarkdown editors enhance note-taking, documentation, and web writing by providing a distraction-free environment with instant formatting. Here are some top choices:вң… Obsidian вҖ“ A powerful, local-first Markdown editor with backlinks, graph views, and plugins for knowledge management. Ideal for writers, researchers, and programmers.вң… VS Code вҖ“ A robust code editor with Markdown preview and extensions.вң… Typora вҖ“ A clean, minimalist editor with real-time rendering.вң… StackEdit вҖ“ A browser-based Markdown editor with cloud sync.рҹ”№ Why Choose Obsidian?Obsidian isnвҖҷt just a Markdown editorвҖ”itвҖҷs a second brain for creatives. With its bi-directional linking, tags, and plugins, it transforms raw notes into a structured knowledge hub.вһЎпёҸ Download Obsidian рҹҡҖ15. ConclusionSwitching to Markdown has revolutionized my learning experience. It allows me to take structured notes, integrate them with GitHub, and focus on learning instead of formatting. Whether youвҖҷre documenting your programming journey or writing blog posts, Markdown is a game-changer!рҹ”— Start your Markdown journey today! вһ•Share your experience below in comments: вһ–Which Markdown Editor do you use and why? вһ–Do you prefer other writing tools over Markdown? why?    вһ–рҹҹЈрҹҹЈрҹҹЈрҹҰҳJump Up High{% include disqus_comments.html %}
